zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert II De Vipont, Baron Vipont of Appleby於1085年出生在法國巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯維額時，他的父親Robert de Vieuxpont35歲，母親Isabel FitzJohn20歲。他於大約1105年與Maude de Curveville在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德阿波萊比結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1153年在英國維斯特莫藍德阿波萊比享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert II De Vipont, Baron Vipont of Appleby于1085年出生在法国巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯維額时，他的父亲Robert de Vieuxpont35岁，母亲Isabel FitzJohn20岁。他于大约1105年与Maude de Curveville在英国英格兰維斯特莫藍德阿波萊比结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1153年在英国維斯特莫藍德阿波萊比享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: Vipont,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Vieuxpont in Calvados, France.

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: Vipont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Vieuxpont in Calvados, France.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Appleby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, and Cumbria, named from Old English æppel or Old Norse epli ‘apple’ + Old Norse bȳ ‘farm’.

variants.name: Applebee
meanings: English: variant of   Appleby  .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Bright’.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert II De Vipont, Baron Vipont of Appleby was born in 1085, in Vieux, Calvados, Lower Normandy, France, his father, Robert de Vieuxpont, was 35 and his mother, Isabel FitzJohn, was 20. He married Maude de Curveville about 1105, in Appleby, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1153, in Appleby, Westmorland, England, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert II De Vipont, Baron Vipont of Appleby est né en 1085, à Vieux, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, France, son père, Robert de Vieuxpont, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Isabel FitzJohn, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Maude de Curveville environ 1105, à Appleby, Westmorland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1153, à Appleby, Westmorland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans.

Er hat um 1105 in Appleby, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom Maude de Curveville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1153 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Appleby, Westmorland, England.

ko.Burgh-by-Sands, Cumberland, England: 영국 잉글랜드 쿠벌랜드 버그 비 샌드즈
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert II De Vipont, Baron Vipont of Appleby(이)가 1085년 프랑스 배세-노먼디 캘바도스 비우스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Vieuxpont(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel FitzJohn(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1105년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 애플레비에서 Maude de Curveville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1153년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 애플레비에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert II De Vipont, Baron Vipont of Appleby nasceu em 1085, em Vieux, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, França, seu pai, Robert de Vieuxpont, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Isabel FitzJohn, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Maude de Curveville aproximadamente 1105, em Appleby, Westmorland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. ru.Burgh-by-Sands, Cumberland, England: бург би сэндс, кумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert II De Vipont, Baron Vipont of Appleby родился в 1085 г. в местности виеукс, калвадос, бассе-норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Robert de Vieuxpont было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel FitzJohn, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Maude de Curveville около 1105 г. в местности эпплеби, вестморланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1153 г. в местности эпплеби, вестморланд, Англия в возрасте 68 лет.

Se casó con Maude de Curveville aproximadamente 1105, en Appleby, Westmorland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos.
